attributes.action: Remove interest from all UK student loans to stop unfair graduate taxation
attributes.additional_details: Student loans now operate like a second tax on graduates, taken directly from pay. High interest means balances grow even while people repay, trapping them for decades. This reduces take-home pay and makes it harder to save, including paying into pensions, leaving graduates paying two forms of tax for education.
attributes.background: Remove interest from all student loans and freeze balances in real terms so repayments reduce debt instead of inflating it. Student loan repayments already function as a graduate tax and should not grow through interest.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition which calls for the same action: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/756832
